column2: phones.csv -- Real public phone numbers (gov, utilities, universities,
hospitals, transport, embassies, banks, airlines) gathered 2026-05-27
from each organization's official site via web search.
Purpose: a dataset DEMO_ENGINE (st/src/rt/demo_eng.cpp::GenCall) can draw
from instead of synthesizing random subscriber digits, so demo runs
produce dial sequences that resolve to named destinations in ph_info.dat
(built from util/inf2dat/{local,ddn,ddi}.inf).
Format: RFC 4180 CSV with all string fields double-quoted.  Lines that
start with '
column3: ' (this header block) are comments -- the consuming parser

column2: in Borland C++ 3.1 skips them with a single `if (line[0]=='
CRLF line endings (enforced by .gitattributes `*.csv text eol=crlf`).
Columns:
category            LOCAL | NAL | INTER
name                Organization / destination (ASCII only).
country             ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.
country_code        ITU-T E.164 country calling code.
region              City / state.  Informational only.
area_code           City or area dial code as dialed from SmartTar.
subscriber          Subscriber digits only, no formatting.
dial_from_smarttar  Exact digit sequence the engine feeds the FSM AFTER
prepending access digits (LOCAL: empty access

column3: INTER: '009'). This is the field GenCall

column2: consumes.
published_number    As shown on the organization's own contact page.
source              URL where the number was found.
notes               Optional context.
Dial-plan note: Colombian numbers are normalized to the 2003-era plan
SmartTar was built for.  Modern Colombian numbers (post-Resolución CRC
5826, 2021) carry a 60+area prefix (604 Antioquia, 601 Bogota/Cundi-
namarca, 605 Caribbean coast).  That prefix is stripped from
dial_from_smarttar so it matches the ph_info.dat numbering tree.  The
published_number column preserves the modern format.
INTER budget note: the engine's INTER_DIGITS=14 leaves 11 digits after
the '009' access.  Some Mexico City entries are 12 digits and lose a
trailing digit when the engine truncates -- the country/city prefix
still matches ddi.inf so call classification is unaffected.

column1: category,"name","country","country_code","region","area_code","subscriber","dial_from_smarttar","published_number","source","notes"
